summary: P1 features verified. Backend: 40/40 pytest tests PASS (100%) covering blog, testimonials, orders/reissue, plus the Stripe checkout-status fallback fix (no longer 500 on initial create). Frontend: home shows testimonials section after audiences and before CTA; /blog lists 3 seeded posts; /blog/:slug detail renders with image and back link; nav shows Journal link; admin dashboard renders 6 tabs (Products(4), Orders(4), Journal(3), Testimonials(3), Messages(3), Bookings(3)); Reissue API call returns 200 with new download_token; Journal/Testimonials new buttons and form fields all have correct data-testid; slug auto-format works.

ui_bugs.component: AdminDashboard Orders tab — Copy link button
ui_bugs.issue: navigator.clipboard.writeText is invoked without try/catch. In contexts where clipboard write permission is denied (e.g., headless/non-secure context), the unhandled promise rejection surfaces a CRA dev redbox ("Uncaught runtime error: Failed to execute 'writeText' on 'Clipboard': Write permission denied"). User flow continues to work and the toast still shows the URL, but the dev overlay blocks subsequent UI interactions during testing. Production users on https with permission won't see the redbox, but a graceful fallback (textarea+execCommand or just toast with link) is recommended.

action_items: Wrap Copy-link clipboard write in try/catch and fall back to a hidden input + document.execCommand('copy') OR show the URL in a toast with a 'Select all' helper, so the dev redbox doesn't appear on permission-denied browsers.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses deprecated @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown'); migrate to lifespan context (carry-over from iter 1).
server.py /api/checkout/status falls back to DB state when Stripe retrieve fails — reasonable; consider also persisting failure metric for observability.
AdminDashboard.jsx Reissue + Copy share clipboard write path; clipboard error is uncaught — fix per action item.

Blog.jsx framer-motion whileInView with initial opacity 0 means cards appear blank until scroll; consider initial opacity 0.001 or staggered fadeIn fallback to avoid empty-looking grid on first paint above-the-fold.
Carry-over: Admin product modal still missing data-testid on individual fields (name/description/price/category/image_url) — flagged in iter 1, not addressed.
seed_data_creation: Backend auto-seeds 3 testimonials and 3 blog posts on startup; tests verified presence and use TEST_-prefixed temporary records that are cleaned up. Reissue paid-order test used the existing paid order from prior webhook test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. The Stripe checkout-status 500 bug from iter 1 is RESOLVED via DB fallback. Admin tab buttons show count suffixes like 'Orders (4)' so use button:has-text('Orders') (substring), not exact match. The clipboard copy in AdminDashboard throws an uncaught promise rejection in headless browsers — workaround: skip the Copy button click during automation, or run in a context with clipboard permission. Slug input on blog form auto-lowercases and replaces non-alphanumerics correctly.
rca_of_the_issue: Iteration 1's only failure (GET /api/checkout/status returning 500 from Stripe retrieve) is now mitigated via try/except around stripe.get_checkout_status with a DB fallback (server.py L478-L521). Verified by TestCheckoutStatusFallback::test_status_returns_200_for_initiated — endpoint now returns 200 with payment_status='unpaid' immediately after session creation, and once webhook marks payment_transactions.payment_status='paid', the same endpoint mints a download_token idempotently.
